vocabulary.definition: A distribution where the left and right sides are approximately mirror images of each other
vocabulary.visual: Heights: 60, 62, 64, 66, 68, 66, 64, 62, 60 — rises and falls evenly like a hill

vocabulary.definition: A distribution where data is bunched on one side with a tail stretching to the other side
vocabulary.visual: Skewed right: most data low (1,2,2,3,3,3,15) — tail stretches toward the high value

vocabulary.definition: A group of data values that are close together
vocabulary.visual: Scores 18, 19, 20, 21, 22 form a cluster around 20 — they are tightly grouped

vocabulary.definition: A large space between data values where no data falls
vocabulary.visual: Data: 5, 6, 7, 8, ___, ___, ___, 20 — the empty space from 9 to 19 is a gap

vocabulary.definition: The shape and spread of a data set
vocabulary.visual: Looking at a dot plot or histogram, you can see if data is symmetric, skewed, clustered, or has gaps

vocabulary.definition: How spread out the values in a data set are
vocabulary.visual: Symmetric data with low variability: tightly packed around the center. Skewed data: spread unevenly

launch.narrative: The league analyst is studying three different data sets from the season: (1) Player heights across all teams, (2) Number of 3-pointers per game by each player, and (3) Years of experience for all coaches. Each data set has a different shape when graphed. Your job is to describe each shape and explain what it tells us about the data.
launch.contextImage: Three different histogram shapes displayed on a sports analytics dashboard
noticePrompts: Do the three data sets look the same when graphed?
Which graph has data bunched in the middle? Which has a long tail?
Are there any gaps or clusters you can spot?
wonderPrompts: Why would different sports data have different shapes?
Does the shape tell you which measure of center to use?

explore.label: Match each sports data description with the shape of its distribution.

cards.text: Player heights: most players are around 5'8"–5'10", with equal numbers taller and shorter
3-pointers per game: most players make 0–2, a few make 3–5, and rarely anyone makes 6+
Free-throw percentage for experienced players: most shoot 70%–90%, a few shoot below 50%
100-meter dash times: most runners are around 12–13 seconds, equal numbers faster and slower
Career home runs: most players hit fewer than 100, a few superstars hit 400+
Senior athletes' reaction times: most are fast (0.2–0.3s), a few are very slow (0.6s+)

discourse.prompt: When data is skewed right, is the mean or median a better measure of center? Why?
discourse.sentenceFrame: When data is skewed right, the tail pulls the ___ toward the ___. The ___ is a better measure because it is not affected by ___. For symmetric data, the mean and median are ___.

approaching.stem: A histogram of basketball players' ages shows: most players are 22–28, with a long tail of older players up to age 40. What is the shape of this distribution?
choices: Skewed right
Symmetric
Skewed left
Uniform
approaching.explanation: Most data is on the left (younger ages) with a tail stretching right (older ages). This is skewed right.

approaching.stem: A data set is symmetric. What is likely true about the mean and median?
choices: They are approximately equal
The mean is much higher than the median
The median is much higher than the mean
They are always exactly the same
approaching.explanation: In a symmetric distribution, the mean and median are approximately equal because the data is balanced on both sides.

approaching.stem: Match each description with the correct distribution shape.

pairs.left: Equal amounts of data on both sides of the center
Most data on the left, tail stretches right
Most data on the right, tail stretches left
Data values grouped tightly with nothing nearby

approaching.stem: Which word describes the direction of the TAIL, not the peak?
choices: Skew — the direction of the long tail tells you the skew direction
Skew — the direction of the peak tells you the skew direction
Skew — the direction the median points
Skew — the direction of the mode
approaching.explanation: The skew direction is named after the TAIL. If the tail stretches to the right, it is skewed right, even though the peak is on the left.

approaching.instructions: Sort each histogram description into its shape.
hints: Symmetric: bars rise and fall evenly from the center. The pattern mirrors itself.
Skewed right: tallest bars on the left, trailing off to the right. Skewed left: opposite.
approaching.label: Sort each bar pattern into the correct distribution shape.

onLevel.stem: A dot plot of goals scored per game shows: 0(5 dots), 1(7 dots), 2(4 dots), 3(2 dots), 4(1 dot). What is the shape?
choices: Skewed right — most values at the low end with a tail to the right
Symmetric — equal on both sides
Skewed left — most values at the high end
Uniform — all bars the same height
onLevel.explanation: The data peaks at 0–1 goals and tails off toward 4. Most data is on the left with fewer values stretching right — this is skewed right.

rows.bestMeasure: Median (not affected by extreme high values)
Median (not affected by extreme low values)

onLevel.label: Complete the table: how does distribution shape affect the relationship between mean and median?

onLevel.stem: Match the data shape with the best measure of center.

pairs.left: Data with a large gap and cluster

onLevel.instructions: For each data set, decide: is it symmetric, skewed right, or skewed left?
hints: Look for outliers or tails. A high outlier creates a right skew. A low outlier creates a left skew.
If data is roughly even on both sides of the center, it is symmetric.
onLevel.label: Classify each data set by its distribution shape.

extending.title: Find the Shape Error

workedExample.label: Shape
workedExample.work: Skewed left because the tall bar is on the right side

workedExample.label: Best measure
workedExample.work: Use the mean because skewed data uses the mean

extending.correctWork: This is skewed RIGHT, not left. The cluster of data is on the left (10–11 seconds) and the tail with the extreme value (14.8) stretches to the right. Also, for skewed data the MEDIAN is the better measure, not the mean, because the mean is pulled toward the tail.
hints: The direction of the skew is the direction of the TAIL, not the peak. Where is the long tail?
The peak is on the left (10–11 seconds) and the tail stretches right to 14.8. That means skewed RIGHT.

extending.prompt: A teacher shows two dot plots of quiz scores from two different classes. Class A's data is symmetric with a cluster around 80. Class B's data is skewed right with most scores between 60–70 and a few scores near 100. Compare the classes: which has a higher median? Which has a higher mean? In which class does the mean better represent a typical score?
extending.sentenceFrame: Class A is ___ with scores clustered around ___. Class B is skewed ___ with most scores at ___. Class A likely has a ___ median. Class B's mean is pulled ___ by the high scores. The mean better represents a typical score in Class ___ because ___.

rows.explanation: Most players are average height, with equal numbers taller and shorter
Most earn modest salaries, but a few superstars earn millions — the tail stretches right
Most scores are high (80–100), but a few bad rounds pull the tail left

extending.label: For each data set, identify the shape, compare mean vs. median, and explain why.

connect.scenario: A sports reporter is writing about marathon finish times. The data shows: most runners finish between 3.5–4.5 hours, with a small group of elite runners finishing in 2.0–2.5 hours, and a long tail of slower runners finishing in 5–7 hours.
connect.promptQuestion: What is the shape of this distribution? Should the reporter use the mean or median to describe a 'typical' finish time?
connect.prompt: The distribution is skewed ___ because the tail stretches toward the ___ finish times. There is a cluster around ___. The reporter should use the ___ because the ___ would be pulled toward the ___ by the extreme values.

exitTicket.stem: A data set has most values clustered between 40–60, with a few values at 90–100. What is the shape of the distribution and which measure of center is best?
choices: Skewed right; use median
Symmetric; use mean
Skewed left; use median
Uniform; use mean
exitTicket.explanation: The data clusters on the left (40–60) with a tail to the right (90–100). This is skewed right. The median is best because the mean would be pulled toward the high values.
